danger_signals.visual_form: Virus-infected hostile robots with distinct mechanical appearances in mine spaces
danger_signals.meaning: Active threat requiring combat engagement; robots patrol and pursue player

danger_signals.visual_form: Central power reactor structure in each mine level
danger_signals.meaning: Critical objective and danger point; destroying it triggers mine self-destruction countdown

danger_signals.visual_form: Visual/audio countdown timer after reactor destruction
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate environmental hazard requiring escape; failure to exit results in death and loss of progress

danger_signals.visual_form: Colored lock indicator displayed in HUD (top-right area)
danger_signals.meaning: Incoming projectile threat from enemy robots; requires evasion or shield management

danger_signals.visual_form: Shield bar in HUD (bottom-left) decreasing with damage taken
danger_signals.meaning: Approaching ship destruction; zero shields = immediate loss of life and hostages

danger_signals.visual_form: Complex 3D tunnel networks with multiple corridors and chambers
danger_signals.meaning: Navigation hazard creating disorientation; environmental threat alongside combat threats

safety_signals.visual_form: Permanent stationary recharge stations within mines
safety_signals.meaning: Safe resupply locations; energy recharges to 100 units for weapons use
Health recovery mechanic; only method to restore shield capacity

safety_signals.visual_form: Door structure that opens after reactor core destruction
safety_signals.meaning: Safe escape route; reaching it before meltdown completes level successfully
Temporary immunity from damage; provides brief combat advantage and safety window
Evasion tool; renders player ship invisible to enemy detection

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Player ship with cockpit perspective HUD; distinct from hostile robots
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Mine exit doors and reactor cores represent player-aligned objectives

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Hostage workers (non-hostile) within mines; visually distinct from combat robots
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Rescue objectives integrated into level design; scattered throughout mine locations

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple robot types with varied mechanical designs; all infected by extraterrestrial computer virus
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Robot generators (spawning points) throughout mines; robot-controlled mine systems

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Mines in standard operational state with active lighting and functional systems; robots patrol normally
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Robots actively hunt player; mine systems operate normally; escape route (exit door) remains sealed

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Visual countdown timer displayed; environmental stress indicators (lighting changes, potential visual distortion)
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Exit door opens automatically; meltdown countdown creates time pressure; robots continue pursuit

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapons scattered at destruction point as collectible power-ups; ship respawns at level start
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All rescued hostages are killed; player resets with baseline equipment; level progress continues but without accumulated bonuses

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Hostage status tracked in player inventory/HUD; visual representation of carried NPCs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Hostages lost if player dies before level completion; rescue provides score bonus for escape

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Spawning structures visible within mine architecture
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Continuous robot generation from these structures; destroyable in advanced difficulty modes

environmental_storytelling.example: Mine architecture across Solar System locations (various planets/moons) communicates world-state of extraterrestrial industrial expansion; player moves through PTMC mining operations to understand scope of viral infection
Reactor cores represent command centers of each mine; their destruction signals player agency in combating the infection; escape sequences communicate urgency and environmental destruction
Trapped PTMC workers within infected mines establish stakes; their rescue or death communicates player choices and consequence

environmental_storytelling.technique: Viral infection manifestation through robot behavior
environmental_storytelling.example: Hostile robot behavior indicates successful viral corruption; robots attack instead of performing normal mining operations, visually demonstrating world-state corruption
Colored access keys (red, blue, yellow inventory display) create progression gating; their collection communicates access to deeper mine sections and escalating danger

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Active pursuit of player; attacks using various weapons; congregate near key objectives
Continuous generation from fixed points; behavior indicates difficulty scaling and reinforcement mechanics
Non-hostile, stationary or limited movement; vulnerable to robot attacks; indicate secondary objectives
Six-degree-of-freedom movement; weapon firing; shield management; evasion of incoming projectiles
Escape-focused behavior required; time-limited movement through mine to exit before meltdown
Stationary, heavily defended by surrounding robots; represents goal state
Triggers environmental meltdown cascade; initiates countdown timer; opens exit pathway

notes: Descent uses a pioneering six-degree-of-freedom movement system combined with true-3D graphics to create spatial danger communication unique among 1995 FPS titles. World-state changes are primarily driven by reactor destruction triggering mine self-destruction—a binary narrative toggle with clear visual and mechanical consequences. Faction distinction relies heavily on behavioral differentiation (hostile robots vs. friendly hostages vs. player agent) rather than visual color-coding. The game's disorientation potential (noted as causing motion sickness in some players) is itself a manifestation of world-state complexity—the labyrinthine 3D environments communicate danger through spatial confusion. Danger signals emphasize environmental hazards (time pressure, navigation complexity) equally with combat threats (robot enemies, incoming missiles).
